summary: If an Allied entry hex is occupied by German units, reinforcements use WAR 21.1.1a Tac mode entry procedure instead.
text: If an Allied entry hex is occupied by German units, any reinforcements due to enter at that hex can be brought on using (WAR 21.1.1a), Reinforcements entering in Tac mode procedure.

title: Map Edge Entry Codes
summary: Reinforcements are grouped by entry areas indicated by codes along map edges. Describes how Allied and German entry codes work.
text:
Reinforcements for a given GT are grouped by entry areas.
• Entry areas are indicated by a code along specific map edges.
• The entry areas are indicated by map edge codes.
• Map edge codes indicate the set of roads where the group may enter.
• German units may enter on any hex on or between the same entry code notation on the map.
• Allied codes are listed within brackets following the group entry.
• Allied entry codes may include more than one entry area code.
• Allied reinforcement entry groups are preceded by a letter (N, W, S) to indicate which side of the map the entry codes of the group lie on.

**Example:** NE [H-I]: 193 FA/VII.

• All US units listed as SW reinforcements may use the primary road that runs from 2733SW to 4333SW as though it ran through full hexes.
• German entry codes are aligned to the three armies.

**Example:** 5P: 9th Panzer Division.

title: Condition Codes
summary: Entries on reinforcement lists may have special condition codes indicated by asterisks, gray highlighting, or green highlighting.
text:
Entries on the reinforcement list may have special conditions associated with them:
  a) If the entry code is marked with an asterisk (*), the unit may not move south or east of the Meuse River until released.
  b) A gray highlighted entry means the unit is part of XXX Corps.
  c) A green highlighted entry means the unit is released from a previous movement restriction.

title: British Reinforcements
summary: All British leg unit reinforcements entering in Strat mode move as Mech units until reaching their assembly points and/or exiting Strat mode.
text:
All British leg unit reinforcements entering in Strat mode move as Mech units until they reach their assembly points (WAR 21.1.1b) and/or exit Strat mode.

**Important:** Units entering using the above rule do not require allocation of TP.

**Designer's Note:** XXX Corps and 21st Army Group used motor assets to move these troops to their Meuse River guarding points.

title: US VIII Corps Art Withdrawal
summary: In scenarios starting Dec 16 or Dec 21, US Art units with VIII Corps designation not assigned to 3rd Army are removed at GT 39.
text:
When playing a scenario or campaign that began on Dec 16, or Dec 21, all in GenS US Art units with a printed VIII Corps designation that are not assigned to the 3rd Army or a corps subordinate to the 3rd Army are removed from the map at the beginning of GT 39 (Dec 28 PM GT).

**Important:** If any of the above units are not in GenS, are eliminated. They do not garner recycle points; however, they may be resurrected (GOSS 22.5.2).

• All withdrawn Art units may reenter the map along any map edge hex on the SW map on or after the Jan 1 AM GT Allied Movement Phase. They may be assigned to 3rd Army or any corps HQ subordinate to the 3rd Army.

title: US Arm Div Withdrawals
summary: Rules governing the withdrawal of US armored division units during the campaign.
text: US Arm Div Withdrawals.

title: CCB/9A Withdrawal
summary: CCB/9Arm units must be withdrawn from the map on the first AM GT after 3rd Army activates, with specific conditions for unit placement and penalties.
text:
CCB/9Arm (including the BU units listed on the Dec 16 set up) must be withdrawn (removed from the map) on the first AM GT after 3rd Army activates (WAR 9.2.2a).
• The units must be at least 2 hexes from the closest German unit and must in be in GenS.

**Important:** Units that do not meet the above criteria must move by the shortest route possible to be able to meet the criteria. These units may not conduct any form of attack or observe for offensive FS missions.

• These units may not return to play.
• For every unit of CCB/9A that was eliminated prior to the withdrawal, one unit of CCA/5A must be withdrawn.
• If all units of CCB/9A (or CCA/5A) are not withdrawn within two GTs, the German side receives 5 VP.

title: US 10th Arm Withdrawal
summary: During Dec 28 PM GT Movement Phase, the Allied player must withdraw all units of the 10th Arm Div meeting specific criteria.
text:
During the Movement Phase of the Dec 28 PM GT, the Allied player must withdraw all units of the 10th Arm Div that:
  1) Can trace a GenS path of any length to their PSS; and,
  2) Are not adjacent to enemy units.
• These units may not be returned to play.
• Units subordinate to the 10th Arm Div that do not meet the requirement in 1 and 2 above, must be moved via the shortest possible route to meet the two requirements.
• The Allied player must withdraw all units that now meet the requirements
• If the Allied player does not withdraw all of the 10th Arm Div by the end of GT 42, the German side receives 5 VP.

**Designer's Note:** US commanders disliked not having a mechanized reserve for each corps command. Additionally, the planners were already thinking ahead to the need for fresh armored divisions in the coming weeks.

title: Replacements
summary: Rules for scheduled replacements, replacement pools, recycle steps, and refit procedures in WaR.
text: 22.0 REPLACEMENTS

title: Scheduled RePs
summary: The TRT lists the number of Inf and Arm RePs each side receives each GT, following GOSS 22.1.1 procedures.
text:
The TRT for each side lists the number of Inf and Arm RePs each side receives each GT. Follow the procedures in GOSS 22.1.1 and the those listed below.

**Important:** Ignore the specialist notation on the German TRT and the Art, Eng, ArmC, Recon notation on the Allied TRT.

title: Allied Armor ReP Pool
summary: During the Replacement Point Segment of Dec 29 (GT 41), the Allied player receives eight Arm RePs with specific usage restrictions.
text:
During the Replacement Point Segment of 29 Dec (GT 41), the Allied player receives eight Arm RePs.
• These RePs must be used during GT 41. They cannot be carried over to any later GT. If not used this GT they are lost.
• These RePs can only be used to rebuild or resurrect US units.

title: British ReP
summary: The British XXX Corps does not receive Inf ReP but maintains a separate ReP pool from US forces, with Arm RePs starting after activation.
text:
The British XXX Corps does not receive Inf ReP (it may recycle Inf step losses as per (WAR 22.2.0)).
• The corps maintains a separate ReP pool from the US forces.
• Beginning the first AM GT after any unit of the British XXX Corps is activated the British receive one Arm ReP each AM GT.

**Important:** British units cannot use US Arm and Inf ReP.

title: Recording Recycle Steps
summary: Both sides track recycle steps as a total for all armies unless scenario instructions state otherwise.
text:
Unless scenario instructions state otherwise, both sides track recycle steps.
• Recycle steps for all armies on each side are recorded as a total. Do not track recycle steps separately for each army.

title: Converting Recycle Steps
summary: Recycle steps are converted to RePs at specified rates for German, US, and British forces.
text:
Recycle steps are converted at the below rates:

**German:**
  Inf: One Inf ReP for every five Inf recycle steps.
  Arm: One Arm ReP for every six Arm recycle steps.

**US:**
  Inf: One Inf ReP for every four Inf recycle steps.
  Arm: One Arm ReP for every three Arm recycle steps.

**British:**
  Inf: One Inf ReP for every five Inf recycle steps.
  Arm: One Arm ReP for every three Arm recycle steps.

title: Refit RePs
summary: Refit RePs are modified: German formations do not receive refit RePs, and Allied non-Arm formations receive one Inf ReP.
text:
Due to the lack of replacements and duration of the campaign, refit RePs are modified as follows:
  a) German formations do not receive refit RePs.
  b) Allied non-Arm formations receive one Inf ReP (both Div and BG).

**Important:** Do not use RE units (GOSS 22.5.1b) or resurrecting with RE units (GOSS 22.6.2c).

title: Rebuilding On-map Units
summary: German units with an attack or defense PR of 9 require double the normal ReP expenditure to replace each step.
text:
German units with an attack or defense PR of 9 require double the normal ReP expenditure to replace each step.

**Example:** The German player is replacing a step of Mot Inf with a defense PR of 9. The player would expend 4 Inf ReP.

title: Resurrecting Units
summary: Eliminated units with either an attack or defense PR of 9 may not be resurrected if eliminated.
text:
Eliminated units with either an attack or defense PR of 9 may not be resurrected if eliminated.

**Important:** German AT, Flak, Art, and PzVI units cannot be resurrected.

title: Leaders
summary: Both sides possess Ldr counters. Leaders not listed in scenario instructions arrive stacked with their assigned HQ.
text:
Both sides possess Ldr counters. Ldrs not listed in the scenario instructions in the at start section arrive stacked with their assigned HQ.

**(Exception: Patton, who arrives on the December 20 Night GT).**

**Important:** Peiper is a formation Ldr, however his benefits only apply to units marked as KG Peiper.

title: Corps & Army Ldrs
summary: Corps and Army leader benefits only apply if the Ldr is activated and moved using the Ldr's activation move in GOSS 23.1.0.
text: **Important:** The below Ldr benefits only apply if the Ldr is activated and moved using the Ldr's activation move in GOSS 23.1.0.

title: German Leaders
summary: Special abilities for German army and corps leaders including Dietrich, Manteuffel, Brandenburg, v.Luettwitz, and Kreuger.
text:
**Dietrich (6th Pz Army):** If Dietrich starts a GT stacked with an SS Div HQ and if all units participating in an offensive GA are subordinate to that Div, the Allied player automatically converts any mandatory PRC result received (asterisk) to a discretionary hit (no PRC).

**Manteuffel (5th Pz Army):** If Manteuffel starts an AM GT stacked with a subordinate corps HQ, one Mech formation (Div or BG) subordinate to that HQ:
  1) Adds one additional MP to all subordinate units for both the AM and PM GT of that GD. This addition is added after adjusting for ground conditions and fuel value; and,
  2) If at least one one-unit subordinate to that formation participates in GA (offensively or defensively) the German player is awarded one favorable column shift. The award may only be used once per GD.
• This award may be in addition to the formation's Ldr benefits.

**Brandenburg (7th Army):** If Brandenburg starts an AM GT stacked with a subordinate formation HQ (Div or BG) and if all units participating in a defensive GA are subordinate to that HQ, the German player may award up to two favorable column shifts to that GA.
• The German player may use both column shifts in one GA or, apply one shift to two defensive GAs.
• The above award may be used each GT if Brandenburg is stacked with the same formation HQ.
• This award cannot combine with other Ldr benefits.

**v.Luettwitz (XLVII Pz Corps):** If vLuettwitz starts an AM GT stacked with a subordinate formation HQ (Div or BG) and if all units participating in the GA are subordinate to that HQ, the German player may award one favorable column shift (attack or defense) to two GAs per GD.
• This award cannot combine with other Ldr benefits.

**Kreuger (LVIII Pz Corps):** If Krueger starts an AM GT stacked with a subordinate formation HQ (Div or BG) and if all units participating in the GA are subordinate to that HQ, the German player may award one favorable column shift (attack or defense) per GD.
• This award may be in addition to the formation's Ldr benefits.

title: Allied Leaders
summary: Special abilities for Allied leaders including Patton and Collins.
text:
**Patton (3rd Army):** If Patton is stacked with a subordinate formation HQ (Div or BG), all that formation's units may advance after GA one additional hex (beyond that conferred by their combat result). The units must be in PA mode and all other maximum advance limits apply.

**Collins (VII Corps):** If Collins starts an AM GT stacked with a subordinate formation HQ (Div or BG), all of that formation's units may perform their movement during a friendly Movement Phase in any order they wish regardless of the type of movement used.

title: Lulls
summary: At the start of Dec 24 AM GT, if a strategic withdrawal has not been declared by Montgomery, players check for mandatory lulls.
text:
At the start of the Dec 24 AM GT if a strategic withdrawal has not yet been declared by Montgomery (WAR 9.2.2a), then players must check to see if a mandatory lull occurs.

**Important:** During the PM and Night GTs of Dec 23, the Allied player must track if a unit of each on map army has been the subject of a German GA.

**Designer's Note:** US V Corps went quiet after Dec 21 and did not go back on the offensive until Jan 14. The German 7th Army only had one corps (LIII) engaged in offensive activity after Dec 21. You cannot be attacking everywhere all the time.

title: When a Mandatory Lull Occurs
summary: During the Joint Command Phase of Dec 24 AM GT, if certain army units were not subject to a GA, the Allied player must declare a corps lull.
text:
During the Joint Command Phase of the Dec 24 AM GT if at least one unit assigned to an army has not been the subject of a GA during the PM and/or Night GT of Dec 23, the Allied player must choose one corps of the affected army(s) and declare a corps lull for that corps.
• The corps selected must have at least two formations assigned. If no such corps exists, the Allied player is free to choose which corps goes into a lull.
• The lull goes into effect immediately.

**Important:** Each Army could be required to declare a corps lull.

• The German player must accept the corps lull.

title: Corps Lull Effects
summary: When a corps is in a mandatory lull, specific restrictions apply to activation, assaults, PR ratings, and unit reassignment.
text:
If a corps is in a mandatory lull, the following effects are in place within the subject corps' ZOP:
  1) Neither side can activate any units/formations (GOSS 24.2.0); and,
  **Exception:** (WAR 24.2.6).
  2) Neither side can conduct PA assaults, and,
  2) Each side may conduct a maximum of two tactical assaults per formation each GT; and,
  3) The offensive PR rating for all Allied units subject to the lull are reduced by one; and,
  4) Units/formations under lull conditions may be reassigned to other corps, however if reassigned those units and/or formations remain under lull effects until the lull ends; and,
  5) Units/formations may be assigned to a corps HQ that is in a lull, however those units and/or formations are immediately subject to all lull rules; and,
  6) The corps boundary of the Allied corps in a lull may not be changed until the first Joint Command Phase after the lull has ended.
• All other lull affects Listed in GOSS 24.2.1 through 24.25 are in effect.

**Exception:** Ignore the instructions in 24.2.4 concerning reinforcements.

title: Third Army Lull Effects
summary: Patton's army is less affected by lulls. A Third Army corps in a lull may still activate one division, and the German player is limited to activating only one formation.
text:
Patton was a much more aggressive commander than any of the other army commanders on the Allied side, therefore his army is less affected by a required lull.
• When a Third Army corps is affected by a lull, that corps one division (Allied player's choice) may be activated (GOSS 24.2.0).
• The German player is side two and may conduct operations as per GOSS 24.2.0.

**Exception:** The German player may activate only one formation.

• The Allied and German player may activate a different formation during each Command Phase during the lull. Ignore the instruction in GOSS 24.2.0 concerning activation.

title: Ending a Lull
summary: Mandatory lulls may be ended in one of three ways during the Joint Command Phase.
text:
Mandatory lulls may be ended in one of three ways during the Joint Command Phase:
  1) During the Command Phase after the lull has been in effect for 15 GTs, the Allied player may (his choice) roll 1d10 during that and each subsequent Command Phase. On a result of 0-4, the lull is ended. On a 5 or greater the lull continues.
  2) If at any time during the lull, any German unit enters the lull corps ZOP, the lull immediately ends.
  3) The Allied player may end the lull at any time by declaring the end of the lull during a Joint Command Phase. The German player receives 5 VP.
